title: Laser Distance Sensor (LDS) Error
shortDescription: The laser distance sensor turret is stuck or blocked and cannot rotate.
fullDescription: Error 1 on Roborock robots indicates that the LDS (Laser Distance Sensor) turret on top of the unit cannot rotate freely. The LDS spins continuously during operation to map the environment using LIDAR. If it is obstructed by debris, hair, or a physical impact, the motor cannot maintain the required rotation speed and the robot halts.

causes.cause: Hair or debris wrapped around the LDS turret base
LDS turret physically blocked by an object or tape
LDS motor bearing worn or seized
LDS motor driver failure on main board

symptoms: Error 1 displayed in the Roborock app
LDS turret does not spin when robot is powered on
Robot starts but immediately stops with error
Grinding or clicking noise from the LDS area

fixSteps.title: Remove debris from LDS turret
fixSteps.instruction: Gently spin the LDS turret on top of the robot with your finger. It should rotate freely. Remove any hair, dust, or debris wrapped around the base of the turret.

fixSteps.title: Clean with compressed air
fixSteps.instruction: Use compressed air around the base of the LDS turret to blow out fine dust and particles that may be restricting rotation.

fixSteps.title: Check for physical damage
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the LDS turret for cracks, warping, or signs of impact. If the turret housing is cracked, it may need replacement.

fixSteps.title: Reboot the robot
fixSteps.instruction: Press and hold the power button for 5 seconds until the robot turns off. Wait 10 seconds and power it back on. This recalibrates the LDS sensor.

whenToCallPro: If the LDS motor or its driver circuit has failed and the turret does not spin even after cleaning.
videoSearchQuery: Roborock Error 1 LDS laser sensor stuck fix

faq.q: What is the LDS sensor on Roborock?
faq.a: The LDS (Laser Distance Sensor) is the rotating turret on top of the robot that uses LIDAR technology to map your home and navigate.

faq.q: Can I manually spin the LDS turret?
faq.a: Yes, it should spin freely with very light force. If it feels stuck or gritty, it needs cleaning or the motor bearing may be worn.

faq.q: Does Error 1 mean my robot is broken?
faq.a: Usually not. Most Error 1 cases are resolved by removing hair and debris from the LDS turret base.

title: Bumper Sensor Stuck
shortDescription: The front bumper is jammed in the pressed position or the sensor is malfunctioning.
fullDescription: Error 2 indicates that the front bumper sensor is reading as constantly pressed. The bumper must spring back freely after contact for the robot to navigate. Debris, sticky residue, or a mechanical jam can prevent proper bumper return.

causes.cause: Debris lodged between the bumper and body
Sticky residue on bumper contact surfaces

symptoms: Bumper does not spring back when pushed
Robot drives backward repeatedly before stopping

fixSteps.title: Tap the bumper around its perimeter
fixSteps.instruction: Firmly tap the bumper at multiple points around its entire circumference to dislodge any trapped debris.

fixSteps.title: Clean the bumper gap
fixSteps.instruction: Use compressed air to blow into the gap between the bumper and robot body. Wipe with a slightly damp cloth to remove residue.

fixSteps.title: Test bumper return
fixSteps.instruction: Press the bumper in at several points and release. It should snap back immediately at all positions.

fixSteps.title: Reboot the robot
fixSteps.instruction: Hold the power button for 5 seconds to shut down, then restart. This clears the sensor state.

whenToCallPro: If the bumper microswitch has failed internally.
videoSearchQuery: Roborock Error 2 bumper stuck fix

faq.q: Why does the bumper keep getting stuck?
faq.a: Fine dust and debris naturally accumulate in the bumper gap during cleaning. Regular cleaning of the gap prevents this.

faq.q: Can I fix a bumper microswitch myself?
faq.a: It requires disassembling the robot and soldering a replacement switch. This is best left to experienced repair technicians.

faq.q: Is a stuck bumper dangerous?
faq.a: No, but the robot cannot navigate safely and will stop to prevent collisions.

title: Wheel Suspended or Stuck
shortDescription: One or both drive wheels are suspended in the air or physically stuck.
fullDescription: Error 3 indicates that the drive wheel sensors detect one or both wheels are not in contact with the floor. This occurs if the robot is lifted, stuck on a ledge, or has a wheel jammed by debris.

causes.cause: Robot stuck on a raised threshold or ledge
Robot has been picked up during operation

symptoms: Robot stopped on a raised surface or edge
One wheel visibly not touching the floor
Wheel does not spring back when pushed

fixSteps.title: Relocate the robot
fixSteps.instruction: Pick up the robot and place it on a flat surface. Press the power button to restart.

fixSteps.title: Clean wheel wells
fixSteps.instruction: Turn the robot over and remove any hair, string, or debris from around both wheel wells. Push each wheel up and down to test the spring.

fixSteps.title: Test wheel springs
fixSteps.instruction: Push each wheel firmly into the body and release. Both should spring back evenly and quickly.

fixSteps.title: Set no-go zones for problem areas
fixSteps.instruction: If the robot repeatedly gets stuck on specific thresholds, set up no-go zones in the Roborock app.

whenToCallPro: If the wheel module spring or motor has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Roborock Error 3 wheel stuck suspended fix

faq.q: Can Roborock climb over thresholds?
faq.a: Most Roborock models can climb thresholds up to 20mm (0.8 inches). Higher thresholds may cause the robot to get stuck.

faq.q: How do I know which wheel is stuck?
faq.a: Turn the robot over and push each wheel. The stuck one will feel stiff or not spring back properly.

faq.q: Does picking up Roborock during cleaning cause Error 3?
faq.a: Yes, lifting the robot triggers the wheel drop sensors. Simply place it back on the floor and restart.

title: Cliff Sensor Error
shortDescription: One or more cliff sensors are detecting a drop-off or are malfunctioning.
fullDescription: Error 4 occurs when the cliff sensors detect a persistent drop-off or are dirty/malfunctioning. The robot stops to prevent falling down stairs or off ledges.

causes.cause: Robot placed near a stairway or ledge
Very dark flooring triggering false detection

symptoms: Robot refuses to move from its location
Robot keeps backing away from certain floor areas
Works on light floors but not dark ones

fixSteps.title: Move to a safe location
fixSteps.instruction: Pick up the robot and place it in the center of a flat floor away from stairs.

fixSteps.title: Clean cliff sensors
fixSteps.instruction: Turn the robot over and wipe all cliff sensor windows along the front edge with a dry microfiber cloth.

fixSteps.title: Test on light flooring
fixSteps.instruction: Run the robot on a light-colored surface. If it works there, the issue is dark-floor false triggering.

fixSteps.title: Use no-go zones
fixSteps.instruction: Set no-go zones in the app around problem areas like stairs or dark-colored rugs.

whenToCallPro: If cliff sensors remain unresponsive after thorough cleaning.
videoSearchQuery: Roborock Error 4 cliff sensor fix dark floor

faq.q: Why does my Roborock avoid black rugs?
faq.a: The infrared cliff sensors interpret very dark surfaces as drop-offs. This is a common limitation of IR-based cliff detection.

faq.q: How many cliff sensors does Roborock have?
faq.a: Most models have 4-6 cliff sensors located along the front and sides of the bottom plate.

faq.q: Can I disable cliff sensors?
faq.a: Roborock does not offer an official option to disable cliff sensors. Some users cover them with tape, but this removes fall protection.

title: Main Brush Stalled
shortDescription: The main brush roller cannot rotate due to an obstruction.
fullDescription: Error 5 indicates that the main brush roller is jammed or stalled. Hair, carpet fibers, and string are the most common culprits wrapping around the brush and its end bearings.

causes.cause: Hair and fibers wrapped around the main brush

symptoms: Main brush not spinning during cleaning
Grinding noise from the brush area
Poor cleaning performance before the error

fixSteps.title: Remove the main brush
fixSteps.instruction: Flip the robot over and release the brush guard clips. Pull the main brush out of the housing.

fixSteps.title: Remove tangled hair
fixSteps.instruction: Cut and remove all hair, string, and fibers from the brush and both end bearings. Clean the bearing caps thoroughly.

fixSteps.title: Clean the brush housing
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe the brush housing cavity and gear area with a dry cloth. Remove any packed debris.

fixSteps.title: Replace worn brush
fixSteps.instruction: If bristles are worn flat or the rubber fins are torn, replace the main brush.

whenToCallPro: Only if the brush motor has failed internally.
videoSearchQuery: Roborock Error 5 main brush stuck hair removal

faq.q: How often should I clean the main brush?
faq.a: Clean the main brush weekly, or more often if you have long-haired pets.

faq.q: Can I wash the main brush with water?
faq.a: Yes, the main brush can be rinsed under running water. Allow it to dry completely before reinstalling.

faq.q: What is the anti-tangle brush?
faq.a: S7 and newer models feature a rubber finned brush that resists hair tangling better than traditional bristle brushes.

title: Side Brush Stalled
shortDescription: The side brush is obstructed and cannot spin.
fullDescription: Error 6 means the side brush motor has detected a stall. Hair and fibers wrapped around the side brush axle are the most common cause.

causes.cause: Hair wrapped around the side brush axle

symptoms: Clicking sound from side brush area

fixSteps.title: Remove the side brush
fixSteps.instruction: Turn the robot over. Unscrew the single Phillips screw holding the side brush and pull it off.

fixSteps.title: Clean the axle
fixSteps.instruction: Remove all hair and fibers from the axle post and from inside the brush mounting hole.

fixSteps.title: Test free rotation
fixSteps.instruction: Spin the exposed axle post by hand. It should turn smoothly. If gritty, apply a drop of silicone lubricant.

fixSteps.title: Reattach with proper tightness
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the side brush and screw. Tighten until snug but the brush can still spin freely.

whenToCallPro: If the side brush motor module has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Roborock Error 6 side brush fix cleaning

faq.q: How often should the side brush be replaced?
faq.a: Replace every 3-6 months or when the bristles become bent or frayed.

faq.q: Does Roborock have one or two side brushes?
faq.a: Most Roborock models have one side brush. Some newer S8 models have dual side brushes.

faq.q: Can I use the robot without a side brush?
faq.a: Yes, but edge and corner cleaning will be significantly reduced.

title: Wheel Motor Error
shortDescription: One or both drive wheel motors are not functioning properly.
fullDescription: Error 7 indicates a drive wheel motor malfunction. The motor is not providing the expected speed or torque, which could be caused by an obstruction, worn motor brushes, or electrical failure.
symptoms: One wheel spins slower than the other
Robot moves erratically or in circles

fixSteps.title: Clean both wheel wells
fixSteps.instruction: Remove hair and debris from both wheel wells. Ensure both wheels spin freely by hand.

fixSteps.title: Test on different surfaces
fixSteps.instruction: Run the robot on both hard floor and carpet. A motor problem will persist on all surfaces.

fixSteps.title: Check wheel module connections
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the bottom plate and ensure both wheel module connectors are firmly seated on the main board.

fixSteps.title: Replace the wheel module
fixSteps.instruction: If one wheel is clearly not functioning, replace that wheel module.

whenToCallPro: If the motor driver circuit on the main board has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Roborock Error 7 wheel motor fix replacement

faq.q: How can I tell which wheel motor is faulty?
faq.a: Lift the robot slightly and power it on. The faulty wheel will not spin or will spin noticeably slower.

faq.q: Are Roborock wheel modules easy to replace?
faq.a: Yes, they are held in with screws and a single connector. It is a straightforward DIY repair.

faq.q: Can a firmware update fix Error 7?
faq.a: Only if the error is caused by a software calibration issue. Hardware motor failures require physical repair.

title: Vacuum Fan Error
shortDescription: The vacuum fan motor is stalled or not reaching the required speed.
fullDescription: Error 8 means the suction fan motor cannot spin at the expected RPM. This is usually caused by a clogged filter, full dustbin, or blocked suction pathway.
symptoms: High-pitched whining from the fan area
Robot feels warm near the dustbin area

fixSteps.title: Empty the dustbin
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the dustbin and empty all debris. Tap it firmly to dislodge compacted dust.

fixSteps.title: Clean or replace the filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the filter and tap it to remove dust. For washable filters, rinse under running water and air dry for 24 hours. Replace if older than 3 months.

fixSteps.title: Check the suction pathway
fixSteps.instruction: Look into the suction intake on the robot and the dustbin intake. Remove any large debris or obstructions.

fixSteps.title: Reboot the robot
fixSteps.instruction: Hold the power button for 5 seconds to shut down, then restart.

whenToCallPro: If the fan motor has physically failed.
videoSearchQuery: Roborock Error 8 fan filter suction fix

faq.q: Can I wash the Roborock filter?
faq.a: Yes, most Roborock filters are washable. Rinse under running water and air dry completely (24 hours) before reinstalling.

faq.q: How often should I replace the filter?
faq.a: Replace every 3-6 months, or sooner if suction is noticeably reduced even after washing.

faq.q: Why does Error 8 happen right after emptying the bin?
faq.a: The filter may be clogged. Even with an empty bin, a dirty filter restricts airflow enough to trigger the error.

title: Dustbin Not Detected
shortDescription: The robot cannot detect the dustbin or it is not properly installed.
fullDescription: Error 9 occurs when the dustbin detection switch or sensor cannot confirm the dustbin is in place. Without the dustbin, the suction pathway is incomplete.

causes.cause: Dustbin detection switch dirty or stuck

symptoms: Dustbin appears properly seated but not recognized

fixSteps.title: Reseat the dustbin
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the dustbin and reinsert it firmly until it clicks into place.

fixSteps.title: Clean the detection contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe the dustbin contacts and the corresponding contacts in the robot with a dry cloth.

fixSteps.title: Check for damage
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the dustbin latch tabs for cracks or damage that could prevent proper seating.

fixSteps.title: Test with a different dustbin
fixSteps.instruction: If available, try a different compatible dustbin to isolate whether the issue is the bin or the robot.

whenToCallPro: If the detection switch on the robot body has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Roborock Error 9 dustbin not detected fix

faq.q: Can I run Roborock without the dustbin?
faq.a: No, the robot will not start without the dustbin detected.

faq.q: Does the auto-empty dock affect dustbin detection?
faq.a: No. Dustbin detection is between the robot and the internal bin. The dock communicates separately.

faq.q: My dustbin clicks in but still shows Error 9. Why?
faq.a: The detection switch may be stuck or dirty. Clean around the bin cavity and try pressing the bin detection button manually with a toothpick.

title: Filter Clogged or Not Installed
shortDescription: The dustbin filter is heavily clogged or missing.
fullDescription: Error 10 specifically indicates that the filter in the dustbin is either missing, improperly installed, or so clogged that it is restricting airflow beyond acceptable limits.

causes.cause: Filter heavily clogged with fine dust
Filter installed incorrectly or upside down
Replacement filter is wrong size or type

symptoms: Fine dust blowing out of the exhaust
Error occurs shortly after starting a cleaning run

fixSteps.title: Clean the filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the filter from the dustbin. Tap it vigorously over a trash can. For washable filters, rinse under running water and air dry for 24 hours.

fixSteps.title: Verify correct installation
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the filter is seated correctly in its slot with the proper orientation. It should fit snugly without gaps.

fixSteps.title: Replace the filter
fixSteps.instruction: If the filter is damaged or remains clogged after washing, install a new replacement filter.

fixSteps.title: Use genuine filters
fixSteps.instruction: Some third-party filters may not fit correctly and can trigger this error. Use Roborock genuine filters for guaranteed compatibility.

whenToCallPro: Not needed for this error. It is always a filter issue.
videoSearchQuery: Roborock Error 10 filter clogged replacement

faq.q: How long does a Roborock filter last?
faq.a: 3-6 months with regular washing. Replace immediately if it is torn or no longer holds shape.

faq.q: Can I run Roborock without a filter?
faq.a: No, the robot requires a filter to function. Running without one would spread fine dust into the air.

faq.q: Why does my filter clog so quickly?
faq.a: Fine dust, pet dander, and construction dust clog filters faster. Consider running the robot more frequently on lower suction to reduce filter load.

title: Magnetic Field Interference
shortDescription: The internal compass is detecting abnormal magnetic fields interfering with navigation.
fullDescription: Error 11 indicates that Roborock's internal magnetometer or compass is experiencing interference from strong magnetic fields. This disrupts the robot's ability to determine its orientation.

causes.cause: Robot near large metal objects or appliances
Strong magnets near the operating area

symptoms: Robot navigation becomes erratic before stopping

fixSteps.title: Move away from magnetic sources
fixSteps.instruction: Relocate the robot away from large appliances, speakers, or metal objects. Try starting it in a different room.

fixSteps.title: Remove magnetic virtual barriers
fixSteps.instruction: If using magnetic barrier strips on the floor, remove them temporarily to test if they are causing interference.

fixSteps.title: Calibrate the compass
fixSteps.instruction: Pick up the robot and slowly rotate it in a figure-8 pattern 3-4 times. This recalibrates the internal compass.

fixSteps.title: Factory reset
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists, perform a factory reset through the app under Settings > Reset.

whenToCallPro: Rarely needed. This is almost always environmental.
videoSearchQuery: Roborock Error 11 compass magnetic interference fix

faq.q: Can refrigerators cause Error 11?
faq.a: Yes, large appliances with motors generate magnetic fields that can interfere with the compass sensor.

faq.q: What is the figure-8 calibration?
faq.a: Holding the robot and moving it in a figure-8 pattern helps the magnetometer recalibrate to the local magnetic environment.

faq.q: Does Error 11 affect the map?
faq.a: Yes, the compass helps with orientation. Interference can cause maps to appear rotated or distorted.

title: Low Battery - Cannot Complete Task
shortDescription: The battery is too low to start or continue the cleaning task.
fullDescription: Error 12 appears when the battery level is too low for the robot to begin a cleaning mission or when it cannot reach the dock to recharge.

causes.cause: Battery depleted during a long cleaning session
Battery not holding charge due to age

symptoms: Robot stops and cannot return to dock
Very short runtime compared to normal

fixSteps.title: Manually dock the robot
fixSteps.instruction: Pick up the robot and place it on the charging dock. Ensure the contacts align and the indicator shows charging.

fixSteps.title: Clean charging contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe the charging contacts on both the robot and dock with a dry cloth.

fixSteps.title: Charge fully before use
fixSteps.instruction: Allow the robot to charge completely (approximately 6 hours from empty) before starting another mission.

fixSteps.title: Replace battery if old
fixSteps.instruction: If the robot is over 2-3 years old and runtime is below 60 minutes, the battery pack needs replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the battery does not charge at all or swells.
videoSearchQuery: Roborock Error 12 low battery fix replacement

faq.q: How long does a Roborock battery last?
faq.a: Typically 2-4 years. The 5200mAh battery provides around 180 minutes of runtime when new.

faq.q: Can Roborock resume cleaning after recharging?
faq.a: Yes, most models support resume-after-charge, automatically continuing where they left off.

faq.q: Should I keep Roborock on the dock when not in use?
faq.a: Yes, keeping it on the dock maintains the battery at optimal charge levels.

title: Charging Fault
shortDescription: The robot is unable to charge properly on the dock.
fullDescription: Error 13 indicates a charging problem. The robot is on the dock but cannot establish a proper charging connection or the charging circuit is not functioning correctly.

causes.cause: Robot not properly aligned on dock

symptoms: Robot sits on dock but does not charge
Charging light blinks abnormally or does not illuminate

fixSteps.title: Clean all contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe the charging contacts on the robot and dock with isopropyl alcohol.

fixSteps.title: Redock the robot
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the robot from the dock, wait 10 seconds, and redock it carefully ensuring proper contact alignment.

fixSteps.title: Check the power adapter
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the dock power adapter is firmly plugged into a working outlet. Try a different outlet.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the dock
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dock for 60 seconds and plug it back in. Wait for it to initialize before docking.

whenToCallPro: If the internal charging circuit has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Roborock Error 13 charging dock fix contacts

faq.q: Where should I place the Roborock dock?
faq.a: On a hard, flat surface against a wall with clear space on both sides and in front.

faq.q: Can I use a different power adapter?
faq.a: Only use the adapter provided by Roborock. Different voltage or amperage can damage the robot.

faq.q: Why does my Roborock keep undocking?
faq.a: The dock may be on a slippery surface. Secure it or place it on a non-slip mat.

title: Battery Temperature Abnormal
shortDescription: The battery temperature is outside the safe operating range.
fullDescription: Error 14 indicates that the battery thermistor has detected a temperature that is too hot or too cold for safe operation or charging.

causes.cause: Robot or dock in direct sunlight or near heat source
Room temperature extremely hot or cold
Battery degraded and generating excess heat

symptoms: Robot refuses to charge or operate
Robot feels very warm or very cold

fixSteps.title: Move to temperate area
fixSteps.instruction: Relocate the robot and dock to an area with a temperature between 50-95F (10-35C). Avoid sunlight, heating vents, and cold garages.

fixSteps.title: Allow temperature to normalize
fixSteps.instruction: Wait 30-60 minutes for the battery temperature to normalize before retrying.

fixSteps.title: Check for battery swelling
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the bottom plate and inspect the battery for any visible swelling or deformation.

fixSteps.title: Replace battery if degraded
fixSteps.instruction: If the battery is more than 3 years old or shows swelling, replace it immediately.

whenToCallPro: If the battery is swollen or the thermistor sensor is faulty.
videoSearchQuery: Roborock Error 14 battery temperature fix

faq.q: Is a swollen battery dangerous?
faq.a: Yes. A swollen lithium battery should be removed carefully and recycled at a battery recycling center. Do not puncture or crush it.

faq.q: Can I store Roborock in a garage?
faq.a: Only if the garage is temperature-controlled. Extreme heat or cold can damage the battery.

faq.q: What is the ideal operating temperature?
faq.a: Roborock operates best between 50-95F (10-35C).

title: Wall Sensor Dirty or Blocked
shortDescription: The wall-following sensor is obstructed or malfunctioning.
fullDescription: Error 15 indicates that the wall sensor (used for following walls and edges) is dirty or blocked. This sensor helps the robot maintain a consistent distance from walls during cleaning.

causes.cause: Dust or debris on the wall sensor window
Sticker or residue covering the sensor
Wall sensor LED or receiver failure

symptoms: Robot bumps into walls harder than normal

fixSteps.title: Clean the wall sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the wall sensor on the right side of the robot. Wipe the sensor window with a dry microfiber cloth.

fixSteps.title: Remove any stickers or obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure nothing is covering or blocking the sensor window, including protective films that may not have been removed.

fixSteps.title: Use compressed air
fixSteps.instruction: Blow compressed air around the wall sensor area to clear fine dust.

fixSteps.title: Reboot
fixSteps.instruction: Hold the power button for 5 seconds to shut down, then restart.

whenToCallPro: If the wall sensor has physically failed.
videoSearchQuery: Roborock Error 15 wall sensor cleaning fix

faq.q: Where is the wall sensor on Roborock?
faq.a: On the right side of the robot body, usually a small window or opening in the side panel.

faq.q: Does the wall sensor affect mapping?
faq.a: It primarily affects wall-following behavior. The LDS laser handles the main mapping.

faq.q: How often should I clean the wall sensor?
faq.a: Weekly, along with your other routine maintenance tasks.

title: Robot Tilted or On Uneven Surface
shortDescription: The accelerometer detects the robot is on a significantly uneven surface.
fullDescription: Error 16 occurs when the internal accelerometer detects excessive tilt, indicating the robot is on a slope, stuck on an object, or tilted beyond safe operating angles.

causes.cause: Robot stuck on a raised object
Thick rug edge or transition strip causing tilt
Sloped flooring exceeding safe tilt angle

symptoms: Robot cannot start on certain floors

fixSteps.title: Move to flat surface
fixSteps.instruction: Pick up the robot and place it on a flat, level floor.

fixSteps.title: Check underneath for stuck objects
fixSteps.instruction: Turn the robot over and check that nothing is caught underneath causing it to sit unevenly.

fixSteps.title: Set no-go zones
fixSteps.instruction: If specific floor transitions cause repeated issues, set no-go zones in the app.

fixSteps.title: Reboot
fixSteps.instruction: Hold the power button for 5 seconds and restart to recalibrate the sensor.

whenToCallPro: If the error occurs on perfectly flat surfaces, indicating sensor failure.
videoSearchQuery: Roborock Error 16 tilt uneven surface fix

faq.q: What tilt angle triggers Error 16?
faq.a: Approximately 15-20 degrees, though the exact threshold varies by model.

faq.q: Can Roborock handle ramps?
faq.a: Gentle ramps under 15 degrees are usually fine. Steeper ramps will trigger the tilt error.

faq.q: Why does my Roborock get stuck on thick rugs?
faq.a: Very thick or plush rugs can cause one wheel to sink lower than the other, triggering the tilt sensor.

title: Map Data Error
shortDescription: The stored map data is corrupted or cannot be loaded.
fullDescription: Error 17 indicates a problem with the stored map data. The map may be corrupted, the storage is full, or the map does not match the current environment significantly enough for the robot to localize itself.

causes.cause: Map data corrupted from a power loss during saving
Significant furniture changes making the map unrecognizable

symptoms: Map appears blank or corrupted in the app
Robot cannot localize and cleans randomly

fixSteps.title: Delete and remap
fixSteps.instruction: In the Roborock app, go to Map Management, delete the corrupted map, and let the robot create a new one.

fixSteps.title: Delete unused maps
fixSteps.instruction: If you have multiple saved maps, delete any unused ones to free storage.

fixSteps.title: Update firmware
fixSteps.instruction: Check for firmware updates that may include map storage improvements.

fixSteps.title: Factory reset
fixSteps.instruction: As a last resort, factory reset the robot through the app to clear all data.

whenToCallPro: If internal flash storage has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Roborock Error 17 map data corrupted fix remap

faq.q: How many maps can Roborock save?
faq.a: Most models support 4 saved maps (one per floor level).

faq.q: Will remapping lose my room labels?
faq.a: Yes. You will need to re-label rooms and reconfigure zones and no-go areas.

faq.q: How long does it take to build a new map?
faq.a: Usually 1-2 cleaning runs to build a complete map.

title: Robot Stuck in Restricted Area
shortDescription: The robot has entered a no-go zone or restricted area and cannot exit.
fullDescription: Error 18 occurs when the robot finds itself in an area that is marked as restricted in the map, or when it has been unable to exit a confined space after multiple attempts.

causes.cause: Robot carried into a no-go zone by someone
Map shift causing misaligned no-go zones
Robot trapped in confined space like under furniture

symptoms: Robot stopped in a no-go zone area
Robot stuck under furniture unable to exit
Repeated failures to complete cleaning runs

fixSteps.title: Manually relocate robot
fixSteps.instruction: Pick up the robot and place it in an open area outside any no-go zones.

fixSteps.title: Adjust no-go zones
fixSteps.instruction: In the app, review and adjust no-go zones. Make sure they are not too tight around the robot's path.

fixSteps.title: Block problem furniture
fixSteps.instruction: If the robot keeps getting trapped under specific furniture, add no-go zones around it or use physical barriers.

fixSteps.title: Update the map
fixSteps.instruction: If the map has shifted, delete it and let the robot remap the area.

whenToCallPro: Not needed. This is always an environmental or configuration issue.
videoSearchQuery: Roborock Error 18 stuck restricted area no-go zone fix

faq.q: Can Roborock get trapped under furniture?
faq.a: Yes, if the clearance is just barely enough for the robot to enter but not navigate out. Use no-go zones around such furniture.

faq.q: Do no-go zones work when the map shifts?
faq.a: If the map shifts, no-go zones may no longer align with the actual room. Delete the map and remap to fix.

faq.q: Can I set up temporary no-go zones?
faq.a: No-go zones in the app are permanent until you remove them. Use virtual walls for temporary barriers.

title: Water Tank Not Installed or Detected
shortDescription: The mopping water tank is missing, empty, or not properly installed.
fullDescription: Error 19 applies to Roborock models with mopping capability. The water tank is not detected, empty, or the tank detection sensor has an issue.

causes.cause: Tank detection magnet or sensor dirty

symptoms: Robot refuses to start mopping mode
No water being dispensed during mopping
Tank appears properly installed but not recognized

fixSteps.title: Reseat the water tank
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the water tank, fill it with clean water, and reinsert it until it clicks.

fixSteps.title: Check the tank seal
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the rubber gasket around the water outlet for damage or debris that could prevent proper seating.

fixSteps.title: Clean the detection area
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe the area where the tank connects to the robot, including any magnets or contact points.

fixSteps.title: Try vacuum-only mode
fixSteps.instruction: If the tank issue cannot be resolved, switch to vacuum-only mode to continue cleaning.

whenToCallPro: If the tank detection sensor on the robot has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Roborock Error 19 water tank not detected fix mopping

faq.q: Can I mop without the water tank?
faq.a: No, the mopping function requires the water tank to be installed and filled.

faq.q: What water should I use?
faq.a: Use clean tap water. Do not add cleaning solutions, detergent, or essential oils to the tank as they can damage the pump.

faq.q: How often should I refill the water tank?
faq.a: The tank typically lasts for one full cleaning session. Refill before each mopping run.

title: Cannot Locate Charging Dock
shortDescription: The robot is unable to find its way back to the charging dock.
fullDescription: Error 20 occurs when the robot has searched for the charging dock but cannot locate it. The dock IR signal may be blocked, the dock moved, or the robot is too far from the dock.

causes.cause: Dock moved to a new location
Objects blocking the dock IR signal
Robot started far from the dock in an unmapped area
Dock IR emitter weak or failing

symptoms: Robot stops in a random location
Robot ran out of battery searching

fixSteps.title: Ensure dock is accessible
fixSteps.instruction: Check that the dock is powered on, has clear space in front, and is against a wall.

fixSteps.title: Clear the dock area
fixSteps.instruction: Remove objects from around the dock that could block the IR signal. Ensure 50cm clear space on each side.

fixSteps.title: Manually dock the robot
fixSteps.instruction: Pick up the robot and place it on the dock to charge.

fixSteps.title: Update the map
fixSteps.instruction: If the dock was moved, update the dock position in the app's map settings.

whenToCallPro: Only if the dock IR emitter has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Roborock Error 20 cannot find dock fix placement

faq.q: Where should the charging dock be placed?
faq.a: Against a flat wall on a hard surface with 50cm clearance on each side and 1.5m in front.

faq.q: Can sunlight affect dock detection?
faq.a: Yes, direct sunlight can interfere with the IR signal. Place the dock away from windows.

faq.q: What if I moved the dock?
faq.a: The robot stores the dock location in its map. Moving the dock requires updating the map.

title: Laser Sensor Blocked by Foreign Object
shortDescription: The LDS laser sensor is partially obstructed, reducing map quality.
fullDescription: Error 21 indicates that while the LDS turret can rotate, the laser beam is partially blocked by a foreign object, dust film, or damage to the transparent cover, reducing the sensor's effective range.

causes.cause: Dust film on the LDS transparent cover
Sticker or tape partially covering the LDS
LDS transparent cover scratched or damaged
Internal dust inside the LDS module

symptoms: Map has missing areas or blind spots
Robot bumps into objects it should detect
Map accuracy reduced compared to normal

fixSteps.title: Clean the LDS cover
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe the transparent window around the LDS turret with a dry microfiber cloth. Remove all dust, fingerprints, and residue.

fixSteps.title: Check for obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure no tape, stickers, or protective film is covering any part of the LDS window.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for scratches
fixSteps.instruction: Look at the LDS window for deep scratches that could refract the laser beam.

fixSteps.title: Blow out internal dust
fixSteps.instruction: Use compressed air around the LDS turret base to remove internal dust buildup.

whenToCallPro: If the LDS cover needs replacement due to deep scratches.
videoSearchQuery: Roborock Error 21 laser sensor blocked cleaning fix

faq.q: How often should I clean the LDS sensor?
faq.a: Wipe the LDS window weekly during regular maintenance.

faq.q: Can scratches on the LDS cover be fixed?
faq.a: Minor scratches can be polished out. Deep scratches require a replacement cover.

faq.q: Does dust inside the LDS module affect performance?
faq.a: Yes, internal dust can reduce the laser's range and accuracy. Professional cleaning may be needed.

title: Internal System Error
shortDescription: A general internal system error has occurred.
fullDescription: Error 22 is a general internal error that does not map to a specific subsystem. It can be caused by firmware glitches, memory errors, or unexpected hardware behavior.
symptoms: Random errors that change on each occurrence

fixSteps.title: Reboot the robot
fixSteps.instruction: Hold the power button for 5 seconds, wait 10 seconds, and turn it back on.

fixSteps.title: Update firmware
fixSteps.instruction: Check for and install any available firmware updates through the Roborock app.

fixSteps.title: Factory reset
fixSteps.instruction: If rebooting and updating do not help, perform a factory reset through Settings > Reset in the app.

fixSteps.title: Contact Roborock support
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists after factory reset, contact Roborock support for diagnosis.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists after factory reset.
videoSearchQuery: Roborock Error 22 internal system error fix reset

faq.q: Is Error 22 serious?
faq.a: Usually not. A reboot resolves most occurrences. It becomes serious if it recurs frequently.

faq.q: Will a factory reset fix Error 22?
faq.a: In most cases, yes. It clears all stored data and resets the firmware to a clean state.

faq.q: Can a firmware update cause Error 22?
faq.a: A failed or interrupted firmware update can cause this. Ensure the robot stays powered and connected during updates.

title: Dock Communication Error
shortDescription: The robot cannot communicate with the auto-empty or multi-function dock.
fullDescription: Error 23 applies to models with smart docks (auto-empty, auto-mop wash). The robot has docked but cannot establish the communication handshake needed for dock functions.

causes.cause: Robot not properly aligned on dock

fixSteps.title: Clean dock contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe the charging and communication contacts on both the robot and dock with isopropyl alcohol.

fixSteps.title: Redock the robot
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and carefully redock the robot, ensuring proper alignment.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the dock
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dock for 60 seconds and plug it back in.

fixSteps.title: Update all firmware
fixSteps.instruction: Update both robot and dock firmware through the app.

whenToCallPro: If the dock communication board has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Roborock Error 23 dock communication auto-empty fix

faq.q: Does Error 23 affect basic charging?
faq.a: Usually not. The robot may still charge but the smart dock features like auto-empty and mop wash will not work.

faq.q: Can I use the robot without the smart dock?
faq.a: Yes, you can use it with a basic charging dock, but you lose auto-empty and mop wash features.

faq.q: How do I update the dock firmware?
faq.a: Dock firmware updates are handled through the Roborock app when the robot is docked and connected to Wi-Fi.

title: Reactive AI Camera Error (MaxV Models)
shortDescription: The front-facing AI obstacle avoidance camera is not functioning.
fullDescription: Error 24 applies to MaxV models with ReactiveAI obstacle avoidance cameras. The front-facing camera used for identifying and avoiding objects is not operational.
symptoms: Robot no longer avoids small objects
Robot bumps into objects it previously avoided

fixSteps.title: Clean the camera lenses
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe both front-facing camera lenses with a dry microfiber cloth.

fixSteps.title: Reboot the robot
fixSteps.instruction: Hold power for 5 seconds to restart and reinitialize the camera system.

fixSteps.title: Update firmware
fixSteps.instruction: ReactiveAI camera improvements are frequently included in firmware updates.

fixSteps.title: Contact Roborock support
fixSteps.instruction: If the camera hardware has failed, professional repair is needed.

whenToCallPro: If the camera module has physically failed.
videoSearchQuery: Roborock MaxV Error 24 camera ReactiveAI fix

faq.q: What is ReactiveAI?
faq.a: ReactiveAI is Roborock's AI-powered obstacle avoidance that uses front cameras to identify and avoid objects like cables, shoes, and pet waste.

faq.q: Can the robot still clean without ReactiveAI?
faq.a: Yes, it will still navigate using the LDS laser sensor, but will not actively avoid small floor objects.

faq.q: Does ReactiveAI work in the dark?
faq.a: Yes, the front cameras have IR illuminators for operation in low-light conditions.

title: Mop Lifting Mechanism Error
shortDescription: The automatic mop lifting mechanism is jammed or not functioning.
fullDescription: Error 25 applies to models with VibraRise or auto-lift mopping. The mechanism that lifts the mop pad when transitioning to carpet is unable to operate, either due to debris or mechanical failure.

causes.cause: Debris preventing mop pad from lifting

symptoms: Mop pad does not lift when approaching carpet
Grinding noise from the mop area
Robot avoids carpet entirely instead of lifting mop

fixSteps.title: Remove and reattach mop pad
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the mop pad and clean the mounting area. Reattach the pad ensuring it seats properly.

fixSteps.title: Clean the lifting mechanism
fixSteps.instruction: Flip the robot and inspect the mop lifting mechanism for trapped debris. Remove any obstructions.

fixSteps.title: Test the lift manually
fixSteps.instruction: With the robot off, gently push the mop plate up and down to check for smooth operation.

fixSteps.title: Reboot the robot
fixSteps.instruction: Restart the robot to recalibrate the lifting mechanism.

whenToCallPro: If the lifting mechanism motor has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Roborock Error 25 mop lifting VibraRise fix

faq.q: How high does the mop lift?
faq.a: VibraRise lifts the mop approximately 5mm on S7 models and up to 10mm on S8 models.

faq.q: Can I disable mop lifting?
faq.a: You can set the robot to vacuum-only mode, which does not use the mop at all.

faq.q: Why does the robot avoid carpet instead of lifting the mop?
faq.a: If the lift mechanism fails, the robot may default to avoiding carpet to prevent wetting it.

title: Mop Vibration Module Error
shortDescription: The sonic vibration mopping module is not functioning properly.
fullDescription: Error 26 indicates that the VibraRise sonic mopping module cannot vibrate at the required frequency. This module scrubs the floor using high-frequency vibrations for deeper cleaning.
symptoms: Mop pad does not vibrate during mopping
Robot mops but without scrubbing action

fixSteps.title: Reattach the mop pad
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and firmly reattach the mop pad to the vibration plate.

fixSteps.title: Clean the vibration plate
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe the mop vibration plate with a damp cloth to remove any debris or residue.

fixSteps.title: Reboot the robot
fixSteps.instruction: Restart the robot to reinitialize the vibration module.

fixSteps.title: Check internal connections
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists, the vibration module connector may need reseating, which requires partial disassembly.

whenToCallPro: If the vibration motor or its driver circuit has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Roborock Error 26 mop vibration VibraRise not working fix

faq.q: What is VibraRise mopping?
faq.a: VibraRise uses high-frequency sonic vibrations (up to 3000 times per minute) to scrub floors while mopping.

faq.q: Can I mop without the vibration feature?
faq.a: The robot will still apply water and drag the mop pad, but the scrubbing action that removes stubborn stains will be absent.

faq.q: How do I know if the vibration is working?
faq.a: Place your hand on the mop plate during mopping. You should feel a distinct vibrating sensation.

title: Auto-Empty Dock Dust Collection Error
shortDescription: The auto-empty dock is unable to vacuum debris from the robot's dustbin.
fullDescription: Error 27 occurs when the auto-empty dock cannot successfully extract debris from the robot's dustbin. The dock's vacuum motor may be blocked, the dust bag full, or the suction pathway obstructed.

causes.cause: Dust bag in the dock is full
Suction pathway between robot and dock blocked

symptoms: Dock does not make the usual loud vacuuming sound
Dock motor runs but bin is not emptied

fixSteps.title: Replace the dust bag
fixSteps.instruction: Open the dock lid and check the dust bag. Replace it if full or nearly full.

fixSteps.title: Check the suction port
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the suction port on the dock and the dustbin outlet on the robot for blockages. Clear any debris.

fixSteps.title: Reseat the robot on dock
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and redock the robot, ensuring proper alignment for the auto-empty suction port.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the dock
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dock for 60 seconds and reconnect. Some docks have a separate power button.

whenToCallPro: If the dock vacuum motor has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Roborock Error 27 auto-empty dock not working fix dust bag

faq.q: How often should I replace the dock dust bag?
faq.a: Every 1-2 months depending on how often the robot runs and the size of your home.

faq.q: Can I reuse the dust bags?
faq.a: Roborock does not recommend reusing bags. However, some users empty and reuse them a few times.

faq.q: Why is the auto-empty so loud?
faq.a: The dock uses a powerful vacuum motor to extract debris from the robot's bin. This is normal and lasts about 10-20 seconds.

title: Dock Water System Error
shortDescription: The multi-function dock's water supply or drainage system is not working.
fullDescription: Error 28 applies to Ultra and Revo docks with mop washing capability. The dock cannot supply clean water or drain dirty water, preventing the mop washing function.
symptoms: Dock does not wash the mop

fixSteps.title: Check water tanks
fixSteps.instruction: Fill the clean water tank and empty the dirty water tank.

fixSteps.title: Clean the water tanks
fixSteps.instruction: Rinse both tanks thoroughly. Check for debris blocking the drain or inlet ports.

fixSteps.title: Clean the dock tray
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and clean the dock wash tray. Check the drain channel for blockages.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the dock
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dock for 60 seconds and reconnect to reset the water system.

whenToCallPro: If the water pump or level sensor has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Roborock Error 28 dock water system mop wash fix Ultra

faq.q: How often should I empty the dirty water tank?
faq.a: After every 2-3 cleaning sessions, or sooner if you notice odors.

faq.q: Can I add cleaning solution to the clean water tank?
faq.a: Only use Roborock-approved floor cleaning solution. Do not use regular detergent or bleach.

faq.q: Why does my dock smell bad?
faq.a: Stagnant dirty water causes odors. Empty the dirty tank after each use and clean the dock tray weekly.
